repo_prior_art.cite: closest is pid_gpn_continuous_rho: fixed-rho continuous GPN inverse threshold, not unconditional Pr(Y1>Y0) over a Spearman band

published_prior_art.cite: FanPark2010DTEBounds gives unrestricted Makarov/DTE bounds; Kellerer1984Duality and BartlKupperLuxPapapantoleon2022 give general marginal/dependence OT duality, but not this causal Spearman-band benefit endpoint
FirpoRidder2019Functionals motivates direct functional bounds; the critical-set statement is mostly definitional rather than a published concordance frontier
FanPark2010DTEBounds gives plug-in endpoint inference for Makarov bounds; this is the analogous sieve transport consistency claim

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_bound_intersection_inference was a failed generic active-set inference warning, not this endpoint-law claim

published_prior_art.cite: ImbensManski2004PartialCI and Stoye2009MoreCI supply interval-ID inference after endpoint limit theory; FanPark2010DTEBounds supplies fewer-than-n bootstrap precedent

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses Bartl-Kupper-Lux-Papapantoleon 2022, a close general dependence-uncertainty/optimal-transport duality paper for Frechet classes with additional joint-information constraints.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The equivalence with R cap R_{1/2}(P) is false under the proposal's own definition of band-level sign determination; band ambiguity only requires inf_R L <= 1/2 <= sup_R U, which need not occur at one rho.

soundness_flags.label: Sanity-check corollaries
soundness_flags.one_line: The unrestricted Spearman corner computes a_- incorrectly: rho=-1 gives E[UV]=1/6, not 0, so the displayed rationale for the vacuous constraint is arithmetically wrong.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The quantile-grid estimator is not fully specified for continuous X and the theorem states consistency without nuisance-rate or grid-bias assumptions sufficient for conditional quantile estimation and sample averaging.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Theorem 1 by the correct band-envelope criterion, or add a single-crossing/connectedness assumption strong enough to make R cap R_{1/2}(P) equivalent to band ambiguity.
Correct the Spearman sanity calculation to use E[UV] in [1/6,1/3] for rho in [-1,1], and note that the standing assumption currently excludes the endpoint values -1 and 1.

Add Bartl, Kupper, Lux, and Papapantoleon 2022 as a closest non-causal OT/dependence-uncertainty comparator and state exactly what the causal Spearman-band benefit endpoint adds beyond their Frechet-class duality results.

Make Theorem 2 conditional on a concrete estimation regime: discrete X with cellwise empirical quantiles, or continuous X with named conditional-quantile nuisance estimators and explicit rates balancing K_n and n.

recommended_next_step: Reject v1 as stated; allow a revised proposal only if the majority-sign theorem is replaced by a correct envelope statement and the estimator theorem is made well posed.
